zh.Warrington, Lancashire, England: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾沃林頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Beatrice Matilda de Middleton於1225年出生在英國藍卡爾韓德麗時，她的父親Henry de Culcheth51歲，母親Baroness Cecilia de Lathom41歲。她和Baron Hugh de Hindley至少有7個兒子。她去世於1307年在她的家鄉享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Beatrice Matilda de Middleton于1225年出生在英国藍卡爾韓德麗时，她的父亲Henry de Culcheth51岁，母亲Baroness Cecilia de Lathom41岁。她和Baron Hugh de Hindley至少有7个儿子。她去世于1307年在她的家乡享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Middleton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so called. In over thirty instances from many different areas, the name is from Old English midel ‘middle’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. However, Middleton on the Hill near Leominster in Herefordshire appears in Domesday Book as Miceltune, the first element clearly being Old English micel ‘large’, ‘great’ (see Middlebrook ). Middleton Baggot and Middleton Priors in Shropshire have early spellings that suggest gem̄thhyll (from gem̄th ‘confluence’ + hyll ‘hill’) + tūn as the origin. This surname is also found in Scotland and Ireland.

History: A Scottish family of this name derives it from lands at Middleto(u)n near Kincardine. The Scottish physician Peter Middleton practiced in New York City after 1752 and was one of the founders of the medical school at King's College (now Columbia University) in 1767. One of the earliest of the Charleston, SC, Middleton family of prominent legislators was Arthur Middleton, born in Charleston in 1681. The name has also been established in Ireland since at least the 13th century; its presence was reinforced by settlers in the 17th century.

variants.name: Midthun
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of about ten farmsteads in western Norway named Midtun, from Old Norse mith ‘middle’ + tún ‘enclosure, farmstead’. Compare Middleton .

variants.name: Melton
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Yorkshire, all of which have the same origin as Middleton , with Old English middel replaced by its Old Norse equivalent methal after the Scandinavian settlement of northern and eastern England.

variants.name: Middlebrook
meanings: English: habitational name from a lost Micklebrook in Blackburn (Lancashire), recorded as Mikelbroc in the 13th century. The placename appears to derive from Old English micel ‘big’ + brōc ‘brook, stream’. The surname has undergone a dissimilatory or folk-etymological change from Mickle- to Middle-.

variants.name: Milton
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the many places called Milton, or sometimes Middleton (see Middleton ) and occasionally Melton (see Melton ), most of which derive from Old English middel ‘middle’ or myln ‘mill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Beatrice Matilda de Middleton was born in 1225, in Hindley, Lancashire, England, her father, Henry de Culcheth, was 51 and her mother, Baroness Cecilia de Lathom, was 41. She had at least 7 sons with Baron Hugh de Hindley. She died in 1307, in her hometown, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Beatrice Matilda de Middleton est née en 1225, à Hindley, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Culcheth, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Baroness Cecilia de Lathom, avait 41 ans. Elle a eu au moins 7 fils avec Baron Hugh de Hindley. Elle est décédée en 1307, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Beatrice Matilda de Middleton 1225 in Hindley, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry de Culcheth 51 und ihre Mutter Baroness Cecilia de Lathom 41. Sie hatte mit Baron Hugh de Hindley mindestens 7 Söhne. Sie starb 1307 im Alter von 82 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Beatrice Matilda de Middleton è nata il nel 1225 nel Hindley, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Henry de Culcheth, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Baroness Cecilia de Lathom, aveva 41 anni. Lei avuto almeno 7 figli con Baron Hugh de Hindley. Morì nel 1307, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 82 anni.

Tuvo por lo menos 7 hijos con Baron Hugh de Hindley. Murió en 1307, en su pueblo, a la edad de 82 años.
